reasoning:
The question asks if Arjun Erigaisi's FIDE ranking on specific dates will be "as high or higher" than on 2026-01-18. His ranking on 2026-01-18 is 5.
1. Jan 25, 2026: FIDE ranking lists are officially updated at the beginning of each month, and Wikipedia aligns with this. Therefore, the list active on Jan 25 will still be the Jan 1 list, leaving his rank firmly at 5. Since 5 is "as high or higher" than 5, this is virtually guaranteed to resolve YES.
2. Feb 17, 2026: This will be based on the official February 1 FIDE list. Arjun has a healthy 15-point rating cushion (2775) over the #6 player (Anish Giri at 2760), minimizing the risk of losing his Top 5 spot. Tata Steel Chess 2026 is the major event occurring in January. If it concludes in February, its results won't affect the February 1 list, meaning his ranking stays #5. If rated early, Arjun maintains excellent form, having won Round 1, gaining him ~4.8 rating points and bringing his cushion to ~20 points over #6. Dropping out of the Top 5 would require a remarkably poor showing combined with excellent performance from those trailing him. Therefore, while more uncertain, he is highly likely to remain 5th or advance to 4th.
